title: campanula pamphylica subsp. pamphylica
intro: Campanula pamphylica subsp. pamphylica, also known as the Cretan bellflower, is a type of flowering plant in the Campanulaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, derived from its roots and leaves. The health benefits of Campanula pamphylica subsp. pamphylica include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. The plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its dried roots can be used in herbal remedies, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine.
